ab: ’inavayan mapiya, piyalralrang na sinsi kaninta mapiya mukuwa i zekalr muzangiya kana dawdawan.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去部落訪問耆老。
en: A: Hello everyone, today I’m taking you to the tribe to interview the elders.

ab: ’inava awa! tarapinuyumayan mi kimazayar kana dawdawan.
ch: B:好喔!我們要跟耆老說卑南語。
en: B: Okay! We’re going to speak Puyuma to the seniors.
A: Are we almost there?
B:Almost there.
A: Grandpa, we’ve come to see you.

ab: kiya! malrazam mu temarapinuyumayan awa! ka’inavayan!
ch: B:你們會說卑南語啊!真是太棒了!
en: B: You guys speak Puyuma! That’s great!

ab: ’inavayan, piyalralrang na sinsi kaninta mukuwa kikaruna kana tinuhusan za dawa.
ch: A:大家好，老師今天要帶大家去小米田工作。
en: A:Hello everyone, today I will take you to work in the millet field.
B: Yes, sir.

ab: alamu uturik mapiya, ikalalup murung za kiyakarukarunan.
ch: A:大家過來排隊，記得帶好工具。
en: A: Come and line up, remember to bring your tools.

ab: ai, sinsi, melak mi lra za kiyakarukarunan.
ch: B:是的，老師，我們都拿好工具了。
en: B: Yes, sir, we’ve got our tools.
A: Are we there yet?
B: Not yet, it’s still far away.
A: You go watering.
A: You go watering.
B: Okay, I’ll water it.
B: Okay, I’ll water it.
A: You go get the bird catcher.

ab: ai, kay ku venawiya za ’ayam inku.
en: B: Okay, I’ll go and catch the birds.

ab: ’inavayan mapiya, auwa ta i tong-u-yēn harem kana wari.
ch: A:大家好，我們今天要去動物園。
en: A: Hello everyone, we are going to the zoo today.

ch: B:好棒喔!我們要怎麼去呢?
en: B: Awesome! How are we going to get there?
A: Let’s take the bus.

ab: uturik mapiya, tratrima’ ta za kippu.
ch: A:大家排好隊，我們準備買票。
en: A: Everyone line up, we are ready to buy tickets.
B: Yes.

ab: i ngawayan mu, a ’aya’ayam, i likuzan mu, a unaunan, uwa karana’una’u mapiya.
ch: A:前面是鳥類，後面是蛇類，大家自己參觀。
en: A: There are birds in the front and snakes in the back.
B: I’m afraid of snakes.

ab: kakuda, uwa na’u za ’ayam nu kemazu.
en: A: It doesn’t matter, then you go and watch the birds.

ab: ta palruwanay za saya tuki zi uwaveliyas ta muwazuk i kani.
ch: A:我們一個小時後回來這裡集合。
en: A: We’ll meet back here in an hour.
B: Yes, thank you.

ab: mena’u u za eman harem kana wari?
en: A: What did you see today?

ab: mena’u ku za sazu za unan zi sazu za ’ayam.
ch: B:我看到了好多蛇，還有好多鳥。
en: B: I saw a lot of snakes and a lot of birds.
A: Hurry up, we have to go.

ab: ngarayu ku ziya, rapi’ ku kuy.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.

ab: ngarayu ku ziya, rapi’ ku kuwi.
en: B: Wait for me, my feet are tired.
A: Shall we go swimming together?
B:Yes! Let’s go!
A: There are a lot of people swimming here.

ab: asavak na zanum i kazu, ’inava nu melranguy ta i kani.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is very deep, so we can swim here.

ab: asavak na ’enay i kazu, ’inava nu melranguy ta i kani.
ch: B:那裡水很深，我們在這裡游泳就好。
en: B: The water is deep there, so we just swim here.
